title: News article snippet
description: Journalistic prose — facts, dates, names at speed
text: On Tuesday morning, scientists at MIT announced a breakthrough in quantum computing that could reshape encryption standards. The research team, led by Dr. Anna Park, published their findings in Nature on May 18, 2026.

tips.text: This is the shape of a news story: short, clear sentences carrying names, places, and dates. I spent a career on copy like this.
Capital letters fill every report - names, titles, places. Practice them at pace until the Shift no longer slows your hand.

tips.target: On Tuesday morning, scientists at MIT announced a breakthrough in quantum computing that could reshape encryption standards.

tips.text: A date is a fact, and a fact gets typed cleanly. Let 18 and 2026 run at the same pace as the words; a wrong figure is worse than a wrong word.

tips.target: The research team, led by Dr. Anna Park, published their findings in Nature on May 18, 2026.

character_tips.anna: News copy is precise by trade. Every fact sits in its place, and you type it as it stands. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.maxim: Learn to type a paragraph like this and no article on any desk will trouble you. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.knopych: A reporter files against the clock. Steady hands beat frantic ones every time. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.klavochka: The day's news, set down plainly and true. That is the whole craft. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
